pattern: Delete every node whose value equals a target by relinking each surviving node's predecessor past the removed ones.
hints: Any node, including the head and several consecutive nodes, may need removing, so the head pointer itself can change.
A dummy node placed before the head means the first node is deleted the same way as any interior node — through its predecessor's next pointer.
Keep a predecessor pointer; when the node ahead matches the target, splice it out by setting predecessor.next to that node's next, otherwise advance the predecessor.

edge_inputs.description: matches scattered through the list including the tail
every node matches, result is empty
leading run of matches removed so head changes

stress.description: very long list with a common target value
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, min(size, 10000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    val = rng.randint(0, 50)
    return [arr, val]
stress.note: small value range guarantees many matches across a long list
